items.q: Why did the Industrial Revolution begin in Britain?
items.correct: A combination of capital from trade and empire, coal and iron deposits, agricultural improvements, political stability, and a culture of invention.
items.misconception: Because James Watt invented the steam engine.
items.random_wrong: Because Britain banned slavery before other countries.
items.why: Inventions are effects of the conditions, not causes. Steam engines existed before Watt; what mattered was the economic and social environment that made them profitable to deploy at scale.

items.q: Why did the American colonies rebel against Britain?
items.correct: Disputes over taxation without representation after the Seven Years' War, growing colonial identity, and British attempts to tighten imperial control on previously autonomous colonies.
items.misconception: The colonists rebelled because they were heavily oppressed and impoverished.
items.random_wrong: Because Britain banned them from owning land west of the Atlantic.
items.why: Americans were actually among the wealthiest and least-taxed people in the empire. The revolt was about principle and political control, not material suffering — a key counter-intuitive point.

items.q: What happened in the Russian Revolution of 1917?
items.correct: There were two revolutions: February overthrew the Tsar and installed the Provisional Government; October saw the Bolsheviks seize power from that government.
items.misconception: The Bolsheviks overthrew the Tsar in 1917.
items.random_wrong: Lenin personally led the storming of the Winter Palace.
items.why: Students collapse 1917 into one event. The Tsar abdicated in February under pressure from liberals and soldiers — the Bolsheviks took power eight months later from a republican government.
It's Hargreaves. “Arkwright” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: When were the Corn Laws repealed?
items.why: Correct answer: 1846. “1832” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: In what year did Stephenson's Rocket win Rainhill Trials?
items.why: The real figure is 1829 AD. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
